title: The VALIS Trilogy — Page 26
raw_text:
Richard Lewis singing it. Deeper, and deeper still.
Once I told Fat that another aria on the record described his mind perfectly.
“Which aria?” Fat said guardedly.
“Total eclipse,” I answered.
 
“Total eclipse! no sun, no moon,
All dark amidst the blaze of noon!
Oh, glorious light! no cheering ray
To glad my eyes with welcome day!
Why thus deprived Thy prime decree?
Sun, moon and stars are dark to me!”
 
To which Fat said, “The opposite is true in my case. I am illuminated by
holy light fired at me from another world. I see what no other man sees.”
He had a point there.
